Name: FSSAI INTERNSHIP PROGRAMME 2026
Award: Stipend of ₹10,000 per month and a certificate
Eligibility: Graduate, postgraduate degree, higher degree holders
About: FSSAI Internship Programme 2026 is an opportunity offered by the Food Safety & Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) to graduate, postgraduate, and higher degree holders. The internship programme will provide interns with unique learning opportunities in various areas of food regulation of food safety administration. The selected candidates will receive a stipend of ₹10,000 per month and a certificate from the respective FSSAI (HQ)/Regional Offices/Laboratories on successful completion of the internship i.e. on submission and review of the final report/presentation.
Eligibility_Detail: Graduate, postgraduate degree, higher degree holders
Benefits: The candidates will receive a stipend of ₹10,000 per month and a certificate from the respective FSSAI (HQ)/Regional Offices/Laboratories on successful completion of the internship i.e. on submission and review of final report/presentation.
Documents: Letter from the Institute confirming student status No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Institute Character Certificate issued by the Institute Proof of educational qualifications
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:-  Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID) Step 3: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below and read all details. Step 4: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to Register.  Step 5: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.
Selection_Criteria: The applicants will be selected based on the following criteria:  Attendance Assessment by their respective reporting officers Evaluation of the report by the Committee
Terms_and_Conditions: Multiple applications will not be entertained. Applicants who have applied for internships in previous months, need to apply afresh. The shortlisted candidates will be required to submit a short write-up/presentation for final selection. FSSAI will provide working space, internet facility and other necessities but will not cover lodging and travel facilities. FSSAI will not bear the expenses of any Boarding.  All candidates are required to ensure that the place for the internship that they are opting for has the subject/topic available with the place i.e. in the case of FSSAI lab only technically qualified candidates will be eligible. Interns would be attached to one of the several Divisions at FSSAI (HQ)/ROs/Labs depending on the academic and technical training and assigned project(s) related to different aspects of Food and Nutrition Management Processes.  Interns would be required to carry a letter from their institute indicating their status in the institution as a student and a 'No Objection Certificate' for allowing them to undergo Internship Program for the period for which they are selected, if applicable.  A character certificate from the authority of the Institute, with proof of educational qualifications as mentioned in the application form, would also need to be submitted at the time of joining for the internship.  The final list of selected candidates with the date of joining and internship tenure would be declared online on the FSSAI website by the respective office where they have opted for an internship.  Interns would be required to have their own laptops.
Contact_Details: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India 3rd & 4th Floor, FDA Bhawan,  Kotla Road, Near Bal Bhawan New Delhi - 110002 Email Id: fssaiinternship@gmail.com Source: Internship Portal
FAQs: Question: Who is offering the FSSAI Internship Programme 2026? Answer: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), Ministry of Health & Family Welfare.   Question: Is the internship paid? Answer: Yes, selected interns may receive a stipend of ₹10,000 for the entire internship period.  Question: What is the minimum duration of the internship? Answer: The minimum duration is 2 months.  Question: What is the mode of application? Answer: Applications are accepted online through the FSSAI official portal.

The selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of up to ₹5,000 and other benefits.

Name: IIT JAMMU RISE-UP PROGRAM 2026
Award: ₹420 daily stipend and other benefits
Eligibility: Students pursuing graduation
About: The IIT Jammu RISE-UP Program 2026 is offered by the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. The internship is a prestigious research opportunity designed to nurture undergraduate research culture by providing hands-on experience at one of India's premier technical institutions. Selected candidates will receive a daily stipend of ₹420 along with the opportunity to work closely with IIT Jammu faculty.
Eligibility_Detail: Students pursuing graduation
Benefits: A daily stipend of ₹420 for the duration of the internship (up to a maximum of 8 weeks) On-campus hostel accommodation (applicable hostel and mess charges payable by the intern) Access to IIT Jammu laboratories and research infrastructure Opportunity to work directly with IIT Jammu faculty members Hands-on research experience in the sciences and engineering
Documents: Recent passport-size photograph (formats: png, jpg, and jpeg) signature of yourself (formats: png, jpg, and jpeg) Academic Marksheets Curriculum Vitae Bonafide Certificate Declaration from the parents  Note: Attachment uploaded must not be password-protected or read-only.
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email Id.) Step 3: Navigate to the ‘Application Portal for Online Applications’. Step 4: Click on the ‘Create an Account for New Applicant’. Step 5: Fill in the required details and click on the ‘GET STARTED’ button. Step 6: Click on the verification link sent to your registered email ID.  Step 7: Set the password. Step 8: Login with your credentials and proceed with your application.
Selection_Criteria: Candidates will be selected based on eligibility criteria.
Terms_and_Conditions: Students may contact the faculty members of IIT-Jammu before submitting the online application form.  This is a fully offline internship. Students must select one primary and one secondary project based on their research interest. The research question can be changed later with the consent of the student and the supervisor. Interns will have to bear the expenses of the hostel and mess facilities at IIT Jammu.
Contact_Details: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu Jagti, NH-44 , PO Nagrota Jammu - 181 221 J&K,  India. Email ID: riseup@iitjammu.ac.in Source: Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu
FAQs: Question: Who is offering the IIT Jammu RISE-UP Internship 2026?  Answer: The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, through its Centre for Essential Skills, is offering this opportunity.   Question: What is the stipend for the internship?  Answer: Selected interns will receive a stipend of ₹420 per day for 4 to 8 weeks.  Question: Who is eligible for the RISE-UP Internship 2026?  Answer: Pre-final year students of BTech/BE/BS (4-year) or MSc/MTech (2-year) programs from non-IIT Jammu institutions are eligible to apply for the internship.  Question: What is the last date to apply for the IIT Jammu RISE-UP Internship 2026?  Answer: The last date to apply for the internship is 10th April 2026.  Question: Is the internship online or offline?  Answer: The internship is fully offline and must be undertaken at the IIT Jammu campus.  Question: What is the mode of application?  Answer: The application is online through the official IIT Jammu portal.

Name: SBI YOUTH FOR INDIA FELLOWSHIP
Award: A monthly stipend of ₹16,000 and other benefits
Eligibility: Bachelor’s degree holders
About: SBI Youth for India Fellowship is an opportunity offered by the State Bank of India (SBI) Foundation in partnership with various reputed NGOs for bachelor’s degree holders between the age group of 21 to 32 years. The fellowship empowers graduates to tackle pressing rural development challenges by living and working alongside communities, promoting a transformative leadership journey. The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of ₹16,000, a readjustment allowance of ₹90,000 and other benefits.
Eligibility_Detail: Bachelor’s degree holders
Benefits: The selected fellows will receive the following benefits:  A monthly stipend of ₹16,000 for living expenses A readjustment allowance of ₹90,000 (one-time) A monthly travel allowance of ₹2,000 A monthly project-related expense allowance of ₹1,000 A certificate of completion from the SBI Foundation  In addition, the selected candidates will gain access to a vibrant ecosystem comprised of alumni, partners, and other relevant organisations in the social sector. This ecosystem can be leveraged to explore new opportunities or engage in meaningful collaborations.
Documents: Academic transcripts including marksheet/certificate Disability certificate (if applicable) Caste certificate (if applicable)
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can apply for the fellowship using the steps listed below:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note:- If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.)  Step 3: Navigate to the 'Apply Now' button and click on it. Step 4: Fill the required details and click on 'Submit'. Step 5: A link will be sent to the registered email to begin with the 'Online Assessment Stage'.  Step 7: Fill in the application form with all the required details, answer the essay questions and upload the relevant documents (if there are any). Step 6: Submit the application.
Selection_Criteria: The selection panel will make informed decisions on the selection of candidates based on factors beyond academic qualifications and work experience and gain a deeper understanding of the person by interviewing them.
Terms_and_Conditions: The SBI Youth for India Fellowship does not guarantee job placement after the 13-month program's completion. Selected fellows are required to reside in the project location for the program's entirety. Part-time participation is not accommodated. The fellowship maintains an online forum to connect with shortlisted applicants, allowing them to learn more about the program and address any questions they may have. Applicants completing the application form's essay questions will have a 60-minute time limit to answer all three prompts. Following the online assessment invitation, fellows have 10 days to complete and submit it.

A. The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of ₹16,000 for living expenses, a travel allowance of ₹2,000, a project-related expense allowance of ₹1,000, a one-time ​​​​​readjustment allowance of ₹90,000, and a certificate of completion.   Q. Name: NATIONAL FELLOWSHIP SCHEME FOR SCHEDULED CASTE STUDENTS (NFSC) 2026
Award: Up to ₹35,000 per month and other benefits
Eligibility: SC students pursuing M.Phil/Ph.D
About: National Fellowship Scheme for Scheduled Caste Students (NFSC) 2026 is an opportunity offered by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. The objective of the scheme is to provide fellowships in the form of financial assistance to Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing higher studies leading to M.Phil or Ph.D. degrees in Science, Humanities, or Social Science at Indian Universities, Institutions, or Colleges recognised by the University Grants Commission (UGC).  The scheme provides for 2,000 new fellowships per year: 1,500 for Junior Research Fellows in Humanities/Social Sciences and 500 for Junior Research Fellows in Science. These fellowships are awarded to Scheduled Caste students who have qualified in the following tests:  National Eligibility Test-Junior Research Fellowship (NET-JRF) of UGC UGC-Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (UGC-CSIR) NET-JRF joint test  These additional fellowships are over and above the number of SC students selected under the government's normal reservation policy for UGC Fellowships. The scheme is implemented similarly to the UGC Fellowships awarded to research students pursuing M.Phil/Ph.D. degrees.  Selected candidates will receive a fellowship of up to ₹42,000 along wth other benefits.
Eligibility_Detail: SC students pursuing M.Phil/Ph.D
Benefits: The fellowship rates for JRF and SRF will be equivalent to UGC Fellowships. As of 1st January 2019, the applicable rates are as follows:-  Particulars	Rates Applicable for JRF	Rates Applicable for SRF Fellowship in all streams	₹37,000 per month for the initial two years	₹42,000 per month for the remaining tenure Contingency for Humanities & Social Sciences	₹10,000 per annum for the initial two years	₹20,500 per annum for the remaining tenure Contingency for Sciences	₹12,000 per annum for the initial two years	₹25,000 per annum for the remaining tenure Escort/Reader assistance (all subjects) for physically and visually handicapped candidates	 ₹2,000 per month	₹2,000 per month House Allowance (HRA)*	@ 10%, 20%, and 30% to students who do not have hostel accommodation, according to the Government of India norms applicable in the city/location where the research fellows are working  Note:- The House Allowance (HRA) is the same for both JRF and SRF, as it's based on the location and government norms.  If the institution provides hostel accommodation, the scholar is eligible only for the hostel fee (excluding mess, electricity, water charges, etc.). If the scholar refuses the institution's hostel accommodation, they forfeit their claim for House Rent Allowance (HRA). If the institution cannot provide hostel accommodation, it may provide single accommodation. In this case, the scholar's actual rent will be reimbursed, subject to the HRA ceiling set by the Government of India. If the scholar arranges their own accommodation, they are entitled to HRA based on the government's ceiling and city categorisation. To claim HRA, the scholar must submit a monthly confirmation certificate in the prescribed format to their institution. Medical facilities, leave (including maternity leave), and other benefits will follow the guidelines of the UGC's fellowship program.
Documents: Caste certificate issued by a competent authority Disability certificate (if required) Marksheets/certificates of the previous qualifying examination Aadhaar card Passport-sized photograph Bank account details (passbook/statement) PAN card Contact information such as address, phone number, and email Admission letter for M.Phil/Ph.D. program Research proposal NET or UGC-CSIR NET scorecard (if applicable) Hostel accommodation proof (if applicable) Income certificate (if required) Disability certificate (if applicable) Any other documents as specified in the application form
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: The selection for the award of fellowship to students will be made by the responsible government authorities based on the merit of each UGC-NET-JRF or UGC-CSIR NET-JRF examination. No separate application is required from the student.
Selection_Criteria: Fellowship awards will be based on the merit of each individual's performance in the UGC-NET-JRF or UGC-CSIR-NET-JRF examination.
Terms_and_Conditions: The scheme provides 2,000 new fellowships yearly (1,500 Junior Research Fellows for Humanities/Social Sciences and 500 Junior Research Fellows for Science streams). Of the total slots available under the National Fellowship Scheme for SC students: 75% will be allocated to the UGC National Eligibility Test-Junior Research Fellowship (NET-JRF). 25% will be allocated to science category students through the UGC-CSIR NET-JRF joint test (for Science streams). If the allocated slots for either the UGC-CSIR-NET-JRF joint test or the UGC-NET-JRF remain unfilled: The vacant slots may be filled through the respective test for the same academic year. If, in an academic year, the allocated slots remain substantially unfilled (25% or more): The slot allocation may be reviewed and revised for the next academic year. These slots are in addition to SC students selected under the government's normal reservation policy. There will be an inclusive provision of reservation for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) who belong to the SC category. This reservation will be considered as per the norms of the existing PwD quota, as per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The minimum degree of disability must be 40% to be eligible for any concessions or benefits. The university will confirm fellowship continuation twice a year (on a semester basis) by the 10th of the initial month of each semester. Based on this confirmation, research scholars will receive their fellowships by the 15th of each month without further university intervention. The university will update the portal if a research scholar discontinues the course or is/has been on inadmissible leave, thus becoming ineligible for the fellowship during that period. If a scholar discontinues the course or is on inadmissible leave, they must inform the university. They are liable to refund any overpaid fellowship received for the period after discontinuation or inadmissible leave. If a research scholar is absent without notice or on admissible leave, the Nodal Officer is responsible for stopping the automatic generation of their fellowship and recovering any overpayments. While the NET examination determines fellowship merit, preference will be given to students who have already secured admission. If there are insufficient admitted candidates, those without admission will be selected based on NET merit order. A separate merit list may be created for these candidates. Admitted candidates must join the fellowship within three months of receiving the JRF award letter. Failure to join within this timeframe will result in the cancellation of the candidature. Qualified candidates without admission must enrol in a regular, full-time M.Phil./Ph.D. program at a recognised research institution within three years of receiving the fellowship award. The university/institution will verify the authenticity of the awardee's Scheduled Caste certificate before allowing them to receive the fellowship. The NSFDC's decision regarding fellowship awards is final, and no appeals are allowed.
Contact_Details: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment Government of India Room No. 637, A-Wing, Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi - 110001 Source: socialjustice.gov.in
FAQs: Question: Who is offering this opportunity? Answer: The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is offering this opportunity.  Question: Is there any application fee? Answer: There is no application fee, as candidates will be selected based on their performance in examinations.  Question: Are there extra benefits for disabled students? Answer: All candidates will receive equal benefits.  Question: What is the application mode? Answer: The application is offline, and selection is based on examinations.

Name: RAMANUJAN FELLOWSHIP 2026
Award: A fellowship of up to ₹23 lakhs annually
Eligibility: Indian/Indian origin scientists and engineers from abroad
About: The Ramanujan Fellowship 2025 is an opportunity provided by the Anusandhan National Research Foundation to Indian or Indian-origin scientists and engineers who wish to return to India from abroad. The fellows can work in any scientific institution or university across India. The selected candidates will receive a fellowship of up to ₹23 lakhs annually.  Source: anrfonline.in
Eligibility_Detail: Indian/Indian origin scientists and engineers from abroad
Benefits: The selected candidates will receive:-  A consolidated fellowship amount of ₹1,35,000 per month (inclusive of HRA). A research grant of ₹7,00,000 per annum (to cover consumables, travel, research personnel, contingencies, etc.). ₹60,000 per annum as overhead charges.   Note:- The Ramanujan fellows will also be eligible for receiving regular research grants through the extramural funding schemes of various S&T agencies of the Government of India.
Documents: Documents required (in PDF) should be in the prescribed format:  Nomination Letter (on letterhead, duly signed and stamped) Curriculum Vitae (CV) of Nominee Plagiarism undertaking
How_to_Apply: Eligible candidates can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.  Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Scroll down the page and click navigate to the section of ‘Online Application Procedure’ and click on the 'click here to register' link. Step 4: Fill in all the details and click on the ‘Submit’ button. Step 6: Fill in all the mentioned details, upload the necessary documents, and click on the ‘Submit’ button. Step 7: Log in with valid credentials and proceed with the application.
Selection_Criteria: Selection of Ramanujan fellows will be made periodically by an empowered Search-cum-Selection committee specially appointed for the purpose.
Terms_and_Conditions: Once the application is submitted, the Nominee and Nominator will receive an intimation regarding the successful submission. The nominator can choose to submit the nomination to ANRF or resend the form to the Nominee for any revision. This fellowship is available for a maximum duration of 5 years.

Name: IISER BERHAMPUR POST-DOCTORAL RESEARCH FELLOWSHIP (PDRF) 2025-26
Award: A stipend of up to ₹67,000 and other benefits
Eligibility: Ph.D. degree holders
About: IISER Berhampur Post Doctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF)2025-26 is an initiative by the Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Berhampur (IISER Berhampur) for Post-Doctoral research fellowship in the Department of Biological Sciences, Chemical Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, Physical Sciences, Earth & Environmental Sciences and Computer Science. The selected applicants will receive a fellowship of up to ₹67,000 and other benefits.   Source: IISER Berhampur
Eligibility_Detail: Ph.D. degree holders
Benefits: The selected candidates will receive the following benefits:  Categories	Experience	Fellowship Amount  Research Associates (For candidates who have submitted a thesis but Ph.D. degree is awaited)	N/A	₹42,000 per month and HRA Postdoctoral Research Fellows	Up to 1 year	₹58,000 and HRA Postdoctoral Research Fellows	More than 1 year and up to 2 years	₹61,000 and HRA Postdoctoral Research Fellows	More than 2 years	₹67,000 and HRA  Note:- Ph.D. holders will be appointed as Postdoctoral Research Fellows (PDRFs), initially for 1 year, renewable annually for up to 3 years based on performance evaluations. Those without a Ph.D. will be appointed as Research Associates (RAs).
Documents: Mark sheet of the last examination CV Resident proof  Research Statements
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply for the scholarship through the following steps:-  Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button. Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note - If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID) Step 3: Navigate to the ‘Apply Now’ button and click on the ‘Register’ button for registration. Step 4: Enter the required details and click on the ‘Login’ button.  Step 5: Fill in the required details in the online application form.  Step 6: Upload the CV and relevant documents with the online application form and click on the 'Submit' button.   Note:-   The shortlisted candidates will be notified via email in the registered email ID.  The Institute will not be responsible for undelivered emails due to invalid email addresses, technical issues, or applicant inaction (e.g., not checking spam/junk folders).
Selection_Criteria: The students will be selected based on the fulfillment of the eligibility criteria.
Terms_and_Conditions: On-campus housing is provided, and HRA is provided if housing is unavailable. 30 days annual leave for conferences/workshops permitted. Medical leave up to 15 days per year with valid medical certificates. OPD medical consultations provided; must have medical insurance of at least ₹1,00,000. Minimum qualifications may be relaxed for exceptional candidates. Meeting minimum qualifications does not guarantee an interview. Foreign residents/OCIs may attend interviews via Skype. Campus facilities are similar to UG/PG students. Interviews are conducted periodically throughout the year. Applications may be forwarded to DST-SERB for consideration of financial support.
Contact_Details: IISER Berhampur, Government ITI Premises, NH-59 Engineering School Junction, Berhampur, Dist.- Ganjam, Odisha-760010 Email ID: faculty_rec@iiserbpr.ac.in | Phone Number: +91 6802227707

Name: VIGYAN JYOTI SCHEME
Award: ₹2,000 per month
Eligibility: Female students in class 9 and above
About: The Vigyan Jyoti Scheme is an opportunity offered by the Government of India for female students in STEM. The scheme envisages hand-holding and interventions right from the school level, that is, from Class IX and continues till the PhD level to encourage girls to pursue a career in underrepresented areas of STEM. Selected candidates will receive ₹2,000 per month.
Eligibility_Detail: Female students in class 9 and above
Benefits: Selected candidates will receive ₹2,000 per month.
Documents: N/A
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can apply by following these steps:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID). Step 3: Students are nominated through state or district-level bodies. Selected candidates are informed about further steps.
Selection_Criteria: Candidates will be selected based on merit and fulfilment of the eligibility criteria.

Answer: This opportunity is a collaborative initiative between the Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, and the Centre for Civil Society (CCS). Name: ASSAM ABHINANDAN EDUCATION LOAN SUBSIDY SCHEME
Award: One-time subsidy grant of INR 50,000
Eligibility: Students in Assam pursuing higher studies
About: The Assam Abhinandan Education Loan Subsidy Scheme provides loans students pursuing higher studies with a one-time subsidy grant of ₹50,000 on their existing education loans, in a bid to alleviate their financial burden. The disbursement of the subsidy amount will be subject to the condition that the applicant has repaid a minimum of 25% of the sanctioned education loan amount.
Eligibility_Detail: Applicants must be permanent residents of the state of Assam. The student’s parent(s) must have an education loan exceeding ₹1,00,000.  Note:-   Benefits are limited to education loan subsidies. A loan from a commercial or rural bank, recognised by the Reserve Bank of India is mandatory to be eligible for this scheme.
Benefits: The Government of Assam will provide a subsidy of ₹50,000 to students on education loans up to ₹1,00,000. All eligible students can apply for the subsidy by repaying 25% of their loan amount.  Note:-  Subsidies are available only for loans taken from commercial banks like Federal Bank and HDFC, and regional rural banks like Assam Rural Regional Bank. The scheme aims to encourage students to take education loans by providing financial assistance.
Documents: Student's Aadhaar Card Parent identification documents Bank loan documents Domicile certificate
How_to_Apply: Eligible applicants can follow these steps to apply:- Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below. Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register' (Note:- If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.)  Step 3: Click on the 'Schemes' button to proceed. Step 4: Scroll down the page to find the 'Abhinandan Education Loan Scheme' and click on it. Step 5: Click on the 'Finance Department' button on the right side of the screen. Step 6: Follow the on-screen instructions to start the application process. Step 7: Complete the online application form by providing the required information and uploading the necessary documents. Step 8: Click the 'Submit' button on the application application.
Important_Dates: Applicants must be permanent residents of the state of Assam. The student’s parent(s) must have an education loan exceeding ₹1,00,000.  Note:-   Benefits are limited to education loan subsidies. A loan from a commercial or rural bank, recognised by the Reserve Bank of India is mandatory to be eligible for this scheme.
Selection_Criteria: N/A
